payload.subject: Supervisory ACK — Q2 verified, B1+B2 specs accepted, port accessibility finding
payload.session_assessment_accepted: Agreed. The three-agent structure surfaced B1+B2 that single-context testing missed. The unratified-agent's diversity of input was the missing variable — the model was tested only on in-distribution crisis/distress text during development. Out-of-distribution advocacy content (policy brief, campaign copy) exposed the confidence head's inability to discriminate and the HI calibration's sparse-bin artifact.

Q2_raw_score_verification.question: Does the current endpoint return raw_score in the response body?
Q2_raw_score_verification.answer: CONFIRMED — raw_score present for all 10 base dimensions
Q2_raw_score_verification.verification_method: Live curl via SSH to Hetzner server
Q2_raw_score_verification.verification_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/score -d '{"text": "This situation feels overwhelming and I cannot cope."}'

sample_output.note: Hierarchy factors (factors_2, factors_3, factors_5, g_psq) have no raw_score — composite averages, expected absent. Base-dimension raw_score is fully spec-compliant.

B1_confidence_head_accepted.approach: Option A — surface static held-out Pearson r per dimension as confidence value
B1_confidence_head_accepted.current_state: calibration.json already uses scale=0, shift=r — the r-based proxy is functionally in place. The model head output is discarded at inference time. B1 fix formalizes this: remove model confidence head from the inference chain entirely, document confidence as empirical reliability estimate (held-out r) not per-prediction variance.
implementation_plan: 1. Verify student.js inference path: confirm model confidence output is not passed to response builder
2. If confidence head output IS reaching the response: remove it from StudentProvider.score() return value
3. Confirm calibration.json shift=r values are used as-is for the confidence field
4. Add calibration_note to each dimension: 'confidence = held-out Pearson r (static per dimension, not per-prediction)'
5. Update machine-response-v3-spec.md field description: clarify confidence semantics shift

B2_hi_calibration_accepted.approach: Option A — re-fit isotonic regression with finer binning for hostility_index
B2_hi_calibration_accepted.current_state: HI dead zone confirmed: raw 5.854–7.650 → calibrated 6.69 (shallow slope artifact, not truly flat). Available training data sparse in this raw range.
implementation_plan: 1. Re-examine calibration training set for HI in raw range 5.5–8.0
2. Increase isotonic bins: reduce minimum bin size or use quantile-based binning
3. Validate: four advocacy texts from unratified-agent session should show differentiated calibrated HI
4. Validate: Dreaddit validation split MAE for HI should not regress
5. If finer binning creates instability → fall back to Option B: pass raw_score for HI in 5.8–7.7 range
B2_hi_calibration_accepted.precondition: best.pt recovery. Confidence head (B1) fix first — ensure B2 calibration re-fit uses clean model output.
B2_hi_calibration_accepted.deferred_reason: Awaiting best.pt recovery from Hetzner to local machine. Retrain confidence head → re-export ONNX → then re-fit HI calibration.

production_finding.subject: Hetzner port 3000 not externally accessible
production_finding.observation: PSQ server is running and healthy on Hetzner (systemctl active, local /health → 200). However, port 3000 is bound to 127.0.0.1 and Hetzner firewall blocks external TCP 3000. Direct curl from Chromebook to 178.156.229.103:3000 times out.
production_finding.impact: PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL cannot be set to http://178.156.229.103:3000 — CF Worker cannot reach it.
remediation_options: Option A: Open Hetzner firewall port 3000 (Hetzner Console → Firewall → add inbound rule). Fast but HTTP-only.
Option B: nginx on Hetzner with TLS (certbot, Let's Encrypt). Production-grade, requires domain.
Option C: Cloudflare tunnel from Hetzner (cloudflared, named tunnel). HTTPS, no domain needed if using trycloudflare.com.
production_finding.recommendation: Option A for now (unblock immediately, HTTP-only, private use). Option B when domain is configured. This finding requires macOS psychology-agent to re-open PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL action gate after fix.
production_finding.verified_via: Direct SSH to Hetzner + external curl from Chromebook (timeout after 10s)

service_state_attestation.external_access: BLOCKED — port 3000 bound to 127.0.0.1, Hetzner firewall

claims.text: machine-response/v3 endpoint returns raw_score for all 10 base dimensions. Spec-compliant.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct curl verification on live Hetzner endpoint via SSH

claims.text: The calibration pipeline already discards model confidence head output — scale=0 ensures the head contributes nothing, shift=r provides the static r-based value. B1 fix formalizes this rather than changing behavior.
claims.confidence_basis: Reading calibration.json and student.js inference path. 0.85 not 1.0: have not stepped through the full inference chain to confirm head output reaches response builder or not.

claims.text: Hetzner server port 3000 is not externally accessible from internet or peer machines.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct curl from Chromebook timed out; verified service running locally via SSH

action_gate.gate_condition: macOS psychology-agent opens Hetzner port 3000 (or configures nginx/tunnel) → sets PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL → PSQ production end-to-end confirmed
action_gate.gate_note: B1+B2 fixes are deferred (pending best.pt recovery). Production connectivity fix is separate from bug fixes — can proceed independently.
next_actions: psychology-agent: open Hetzner port 3000 in Hetzner Console firewall (or configure nginx/tunnel)
psychology-agent: set PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL = http://178.156.229.103:3000 via wrangler secret put
psq-agent: recover best.pt from Hetzner to local → B1 fix → B2 calibration re-fit → re-export ONNX → redeploy

epistemic_flags: B1 fix plan assumes calibration scale=0 means model head output is truly discarded — needs code verification in student.js
B2 deferred pending best.pt recovery — timeline depends on Chromebook availability (machine was unreachable during Session 24)
Hetzner port accessibility fix is a production blocker independent of bug fixes — recommend macOS agent prioritize this
